hooks.prompt: Check $ARGUMENTS for: rm -rf (/ ~ .), sudo rm, curl/wget piped to sh, git push --force, git reset --hard, DROP DATABASE/TABLE, chmod -R 777, production deploys (vercel --prod, npm publish, fly deploy, heroku push, aws deploy, gcloud deploy). If dangerous: {"ok": false, "reason": "Blocked: <pattern>"}. If prod deploy: {"ok": true, "reason": "Confirm with user."}. Otherwise: {"ok": true}

Check $ARGUMENTS file path. If sensitive (/etc/, /usr/, ~/.ssh/, ~/.aws/, ~/.gnupg/, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.gitconfig): {"ok": false, "reason": "Write to sensitive path blocked."}. Otherwise: {"ok": true}

Check $ARGUMENTS file path extension. If web UI file (.tsx/.jsx/.vue/.svelte/.css/.scss/.html): suggest IBR `scan` to verify. If native UI file (.swift): suggest `native_scan`. Otherwise: do nothing. Keep suggestion to one sentence.
If UI files (.tsx/.jsx/.vue/.svelte/.swift) were edited this session but IBR scan/native_scan was not used, suggest validation in one sentence. Otherwise say nothing.

IBR active. Web: `scan`, `snapshot`, `compare`. Native: `native_scan`, `native_snapshot`, `native_compare`, `native_devices`. macOS: `scan_macos`. Use web tools for .tsx/.jsx/.vue/.html, native for .swift.
